actionUnits.condition: Validation step: Confirm the MonoBehaviour on this GameObject corresponds to Fire.cs (m_Script guid: 1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d) and uses the exact serialized settings provided (debug=0, isOn=0, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60, maxHitInterval=2, totalHitTime=5, onNeighborsThreshold=2, neighbors=[]). This confirms the scene MonoBehaviour matches the attached script and settings.

Precondition to enable Off->On/On->Off logic: Fire must start On. Externally set Fire.SetIsOn(true) at runtime (this method takes a bool parameter; the current harness cannot call parameterized methods). Expected: UpdateFireState(false) is invoked internally; mainEffectGo ParticleSystem plays.

Simulate a single water hit on the collider (BoxCollider enabled) via the water system calling IWaterInteractable.WaterHit(Vector3 normal). This sets isTurningOff=true and resets currentHitInterval=0 while fire is On. Repeat hits should occur within maxHitInterval (2s).
Second water hit within 2s window (keep extinguish timer accumulating). CurrentHitTime should be ~2s now, isTurningOff remains true.
Third water hit within 2s window (continue extinguish). CurrentHitTime ~3s.

Stop water for > maxHitInterval (2s). Expected: In Update(), since currentHitInterval >= maxHitInterval, isTurningOff becomes false and currentHitTime resets to 0. This covers the early-abort branch of the turning-off logic.
Re-enable fire to On again via external call: Fire.SetIsOn(true) (parameterized; requires test driver). Expected: ParticleSystem plays.
Water hit within 2s window (continue to 2s total).
Water hit within 2s window (continue to 3s total).
Water hit within 2s window (continue to 4s total).

Water hit within 2s window (reach totalHitTime=5s). Expected: Fire turns off (isOn=false), shouldTurnOn=false, timeToOnDelta=0, isTurningOff=false, currentHitTime=0. UpdateFireState(true) executes, stopping the ParticleSystem and notifying neighbors (if any) and FireController.Instance.FireChanged(-1).

Neighbor-based auto-ignition test setup (requires neighbors): Two distinct neighbor Fire instances within maxNeighborDistance should notify this fire via NotifyNeighborState(true) so that onNeighbors >= onNeighborsThreshold (2). This sets shouldTurnOn=true, timeToOnDelta=0, and computes hasDownNeighbor. Note: This requires parameterized calls and neighbor setup not available in this harness.

With shouldTurnOn=true, wait until timeToOnDelta >= timeToOn (Awake sets timeToOn to Random.Range(timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60)). After ~61s, expected state transition to isOn=true; UpdateFireState(true) plays particles and notifies neighbors and FireController. If a down neighbor exists (y lower by > minNeighborDownDistance), the downMultiplier affects the countdown speed.
